Title: Login to the UniMove application
Preconditions: User has the UniMove application installed on their device.
Test Steps:
1. Open the UniMove application.
2. Tap on the "Login" button.
3. Enter the valid username and password.
4. Tap on the "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in to the UniMove application.

Title: Create a new account in the UniMove application
Preconditions: User does not have an account in the UniMove application.
Test Steps:
1. Open the UniMove application.
2. Tap on the "Sign Up" button.
3. Enter the valid username, email, and password.
4. Tap on the "Create Account" button.
Expected Result: User is successfully registered and logged in to the UniMove application.

Title: Account creation
Preconditions: The user has installed the app on their device.
Test Steps:
1. The user opens the app for the first time.
2. The user enters their email and password.
3. The user confirms the password by re-entering it.
4. The user clicks on the link sent to their email to confirm ownership.
Expected Result: The account is registered in the database and the user is granted access to the application and its features.

Title: Login
Preconditions: The user has installed the app on their device and has created an account.
Test Steps:
1. The user opens the app.
2. The user enters their email and password.
3. The user clicks on the "Login" button.
Expected Result: The user is authenticated and can access the application.

Title: Calendar
Preconditions: The user has logged in to their account.
Test Steps: 1. The user clicks on the calendar button.
Expected Result: The user is taken to the calendar page.

Title: View schedule
Preconditions: The user is on the calendar page.
Test Steps: 1. The user views the schedule.
Expected Result: The user can see the schedule they have entered.

Title: Modify schedule
Preconditions: The user is on the calendar page.
Test Steps:
1. The user clicks on the "Modify schedule" button.
2. The user removes courses they are not attending or adds new ones.
3. The user confirms the changes by clicking on the appropriate button.
Expected Result: If the user has made changes, these changes are permanent. If the user does not confirm the changes, they are not saved.

Title: View train schedule
Preconditions: The user is on the calendar page.
Test Steps: 1. The user views the train schedule.
Expected Result: The user can see the train schedule with all the relevant information.

Title: View variations
Preconditions: The user has logged in to their account.
Test Steps: 1. The user clicks on the "Variations" button.
Expected Result: The user can see the information about the variations of the trains.

Title: Report a problem
Preconditions: The user has logged in to their account.
Test Steps:
1. The user clicks on the "Report a problem" button.
2. The user selects the appropriate option in the "Report a problem" page.
3. The user describes the problem, including specific details and the type of report.
4. The user confirms and sends the report.
Expected Result: The reports are saved and inserted into the database.

Title: View reports
Preconditions: The user has logged in to their account.
Test Steps: 1. The user clicks on the "View reports" button.
Expected Result: The user can see the list of reports.

Title: View Profile
Test Steps:
1. User selects the "Profile" option from the main screen.
2. User navigates to the "Profile" page.
3. User views their personal information.
Expected Result: User can view their personal information.

Title: Edit Profile
Preconditions: User has logged in and is on the "Profile" page.
Test Steps:
1. User selects the "Edit" option on the "Profile" page.
2. User is presented with a pre-filled form with their current information.
3. User modifies their personal information.
4. User confirms the changes.
Expected Result: User's personal information is updated.

Title: Change Location
Preconditions: User has logged in and is on the "Profile" page.
Test Steps:
1. User selects the "Edit" option on the "Profile" page.
2. User is presented with a form to modify their location.
3. User enters a new location.
4. User confirms the changes.
Expected Result: User's location is updated.

Title: Notification
Test Steps:
1. There is a change in the user's schedule or transportation method.
2. The user receives a notification on their device.
Expected Result: User receives a notification about the change.

Title: Logout
Test Steps:
1. User selects the "Logout" option.
2. User is redirected to the login screen.
Expected Result: User is logged out.

Title: Check if the application calculates and informs the user about the time needed to reach the train station.
Preconditions: User has entered their destination and the current time.
Test Steps:
1. Enter the destination and current time.
2. Click on the "Calculate" button.
3. Check the displayed time needed to reach the train station.
Expected Result: The application displays the time needed to reach the train station.

Title: Check if the application allows the user to select trains for reaching and leaving the place of study/work on different days of the week.
Preconditions: User has entered their place of study/work and the days of the week.
Test Data: Place of study/work and days of the week.
Test Steps:
1. Enter the place of study/work and the days of the week.
2. Click on the "Search" button.
3. Check the list of possible train solutions for each day of the week.
Expected Result: The application displays a list of possible train solutions for each day of the week.

Title: Check if the application allows the user to visualize their personal data, including the trains selected and the work/study schedules.
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Click on the "Profile" button.
3. Check the displayed personal data, including the trains selected and the work/study schedules.
Expected Result: The application displays the user's personal data, including the trains selected and the work/study schedules.

Title: Check if the application provides the user with information about the selected train, including the train identifier, type, departure and arrival stations, and intermediate stations with their respective departure and arrival times, as well as information about possible delays.
Test Steps:
1. Select a train from the list of available trains.
2. Check the displayed information about the selected train.
Expected Result: The application displays the information about the selected train, including the train identifier, type, departure and arrival stations, and intermediate stations with their respective departure and arrival times, as well as information about possible delays.

Title: Check if the application allows the user to create a complaint about possible problems with the trains.
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Click on the "Create Complaint" button.
3. Enter the description of the problem.
4. Click on the "Submit" button.
Expected Result: The complaint is created and saved in the database.

Title: Check if the application allows the user to view the various complaints.
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Click on the "Complaints" button.
3. Check the displayed complaints.
Expected Result: The application displays the various complaints.

Title: Check if the application allows the user to visualize their own schedule, including the departure and arrival times of the trains they have selected.
Preconditions: User has selected their departure and arrival stations and the desired departure time.
Test Data: Departure and arrival stations and the desired departure time.
Test Steps:
1. Enter the departure and arrival stations and the desired departure time.
2. Click on the "Search" button.
3. Check the displayed list of possible solutions for reaching the destination in the shortest time possible.
Expected Result: The application displays a list of possible solutions for reaching the destination in the shortest time possible.

Title: Check if the application keeps the user informed about possible train variations.
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Click on the "Variations" button.
3. Check if the application displays any information about possible train variations.
Expected Result: The application displays information about possible train variations.

Title: Check if the application allows the user to visualize the possible train variations and/or cancellations.
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Click on the "Variations" button.
3. Check the displayed possible train variations and/or cancellations.
Expected Result: The application displays the possible train variations and/or cancellations.

Title: Check if the application allows the user to access the pages where they can view, modify, or insert their own schedules.
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Click on the "Schedules" button.
3. Check if the user can view, modify, or insert their own schedules.
Expected Result: The user can view, modify, or insert their own schedules.

Title: Check if the application allows the user to visualize their own schedules, including the times of their classes or work.
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Click on the "Schedules" button.
3. Check if the user can view their own schedules, including the times of their classes or work.
Expected Result: The user can view their own schedules, including the times of their classes or work.

Title: Check if the application allows the user to personalize their profile by entering their place of work/study.
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Click on the "Profile" button.
3. Click on the "Edit" button.
4. Enter the place of work/study.
5. Click on the "Save" button.
Expected Result: The user's data, including the place of work/study, is saved in the database.

Title: Check if the application allows the user to visualize the various trains available based on the entered information, such as the time and place of work/study.
Preconditions: User has entered their place of work/study and the desired departure time.
Test Data: Place of work/study and the desired departure time.
Test Steps:
1. Enter the place of work/study and the desired departure time.
2. Click on the "Search" button.
3. Check the displayed list of available trains.
Expected Result: The application displays the list of available trains based on the entered information.

Title: Check if the application allows the user to access the page where they can view the information about the trains.
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Click on the "Trains" button.
3. Check if the user can view the information about the trains.
Expected Result: The user can view the information about the trains.

Title: Check if the application keeps the user informed about changes in the trains they have selected or with which they will travel during the day.
Preconditions: User has selected their preferred trains or trains they will travel with during the day.
Test Steps:
1. Select the preferred trains or trains they will travel with during the day.
2. Check if the user receives notifications about changes in the selected trains or trains they will travel with during the day.
Expected Result: The user receives notifications about changes in the selected trains or trains they will travel with during the day.

Title: Simple and intuitive user interface
Preconditions: User has completed a 30-minute training session
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application
2. Navigate through the application's screens
3. Observe the user interface elements and their interactions
Expected Result: The user interface should be simple and intuitive, allowing the user to navigate through the application without committing errors.

Title: No use of animations with intermittent lights
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application
2. Observe the animations used in the application
Expected Result: The application should not use animations with intermittent lights to ensure inclusivity for a wider range of users.

Title: Help page with instructions
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application
2. Access the help page
Expected Result: The help page should contain instructions necessary for the user to utilize the application effectively.

Title: Maximum storage space of 100 MB
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on a device
2. Monitor the storage space used by the application
Expected Result: The application should occupy no more than 100 MB of storage space.

Title: Responsive system
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application
2. Perform various actions in the application
3. Measure the time taken for the application to respond to user requests
Expected Result: The application should be reactive, with a maximum response time of 10 seconds for the best route and 1 second for screen changes.

Title: System stability
Test Steps: 1. Use the application for an extended period
Expected Result: The system should function correctly without frequent crashes (no more than 1-2 crashes per month).

Title: Data synchronization across devices
Preconditions: User has logged in to the application on multiple devices
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application on one device
2. Modify user data and settings on that device
3. Log in to the application on another device
4. Observe if the modified data and settings are present on the second device
Expected Result: The user should be able to access their data and settings from different devices by logging in with their credentials, ensuring data transparency across devices.

Title: Meeting project deadlines
Test Steps: 1. Monitor the delivery of project deliverables
Expected Result: Deliverables should be delivered on time to ensure the successful completion of the project.

Title: Use of Android Studio and Java for development
Test Steps: 1. Analyze the development environment used for the application
Expected Result: The application should be developed using Android Studio and Java, as specified in the project requirements.

Title: Firebase Database Usage
Test Steps:
1. Open the application
2. Check if Firebase is being used as the database
Expected Result: Firebase is being used as the database

Title: GDPR Compliance for User Data
Test Steps:
1. Collect user data
2. Check if user data is being handled according to GDPR
Expected Result: User data is being handled according to GDPR

Title: Minimum Necessary Data Provided to Trenitalia's ViaggiaTreno APIs
Test Steps:
1. Send a request to Trenitalia's ViaggiaTreno APIs
2. Check if only the minimum necessary data is being sent
Expected Result: Only the minimum necessary data is being sent to Trenitalia's ViaggiaTreno APIs

Title: Internet Connection Requirement
Test Steps:
1. Check if the application requires a constant internet connection
2. Disconnect the internet
3. Check if the application crashes or stops working
Expected Result: The application requires a constant internet connection and does not crash or stop working without it

Title: Application Size
Test Steps:
1. Check the size of the application
2. Compare the size with the specified limit (100 MB)
Expected Result: The application size is less than or equal to 100 MB

Title: Application Response Time
Test Steps:
1. Send a request to the application
2. Measure the time taken for the application to respond
Expected Result: The time taken for the application to respond is less than or equal to 10 seconds

Title: Application Usability
Test Steps:
1. Use the application for 30 minutes
2. Check if the user can use the application without committing errors
Expected Result: The user can use the application without committing errors after a 30-minute training

Title: Absence of Intermittent Light Animations
Test Steps:
1. Open the application
2. Check if the application does not use animations with intermittent lights
Expected Result: The application does not use animations with intermittent lights

Title: Presence of Help Page
Test Steps:
1. Open the application
2. Check if a help page is available
Expected Result: A help page is available in the application

Title: Reduced Crash Rate
Test Steps:
1. Use the application for a significant amount of time
2. Check if the crash rate is reduced
Expected Result: The crash rate is reduced

Title: Correct Application Installation
Test Steps:
1. Install the application
2. Check if the installation is successful
Expected Result: The application installation is successful

Title: User Data Removal on Uninstall
Test Steps:
1. Uninstall the application
2. Check if all user data is removed from the device
Expected Result: All user data is removed from the device when the application is uninstalled

Title: Compatibility with Android 7.0 or Higher
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on a device with Android 7.0 or higher
2. Check if the application functions correctly
Expected Result: The application functions correctly on a device with Android 7.0 or higher

Test Steps:

Expected Result: A version of the application is developed for a different platform

Expected Result: The offline functionality with caching works correctly

2. Test the application in multiple languages
Expected Result: The application works correctly in multiple languages

Title: Integration with University Applications (Future Feature)

Test Steps:

Title: Search for trains based on user-specified origin and destination
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the appropriate page.
Test Data: Origin: Rome, Destination: Milan, Desired Departure Time: 10:00 AM
Test Steps:
1. User enters the desired departure time and origin and destination stations.
2. User clicks the search button.
Expected Result: The user receives a list of trains that depart from the specified origin at the specified time and arrive at the specified destination.

Title: Verify Firebase Database Integration
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application.
2. Navigate to the section where the database is supposed to be used.
3. Check if Firebase is being used as the database.
Expected Result: The application should be using Firebase as the database.

Title: Verify Minimum Android Version Requirement
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application on an Android device.
2. Check the Android version of the device.
Expected Result: The Android version of the device should be 7.0 or later.

Title: Verify Minimum RAM Requirement
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application on a device with at least 1 GB of RAM.
2. Check the available RAM on the device.
Expected Result: The device should have at least 1 GB of RAM.

Title: Verify Minimum Storage Requirement
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application on a device with at least 100 MB of free storage.
2. Check the available free storage on the device.
Expected Result: The device should have at least 100 MB of free storage.
